name: Lemongrass Chicken Wip
recipeIngredient: 1 Frying Chicken - cut into serving pieces
3 Scallion (green and white parts), finely minced
2 small Chili Peppers (red or green Thai peppers)
1/2 cup Chinese Chives (onion chives), cut into 2-inch pieces
2 tbsp Fish Sauce (Nuoc Mam or Nam Pla)
recipeInstructions:
Notes:
A. Lemongrass: Remove outer leaves and bruise the white end with a knife or wine bottle. Slice the tender white part at the base of the stalk; discard the tops unless saved for chicken stock.
B. Fish Sauce: A good brand is "Tiparos" from Thailand.
1. Mix chicken pieces with lemongrass, scallions, vinegar, Nuoc Mam, salt, and pepper. Set aside for at least 30 minutes; overnight is better.
2. If using thick slices of lemongrass, remove them before cooking in the wok; if thin, they can be cooked along with the chicken.
3. Heat oil in a wok, add chiles, then add chicken pieces. Stir and cook for 3 or more minutes until chicken is no longer pink. Season with sugar and chili or white pepper to taste.
4. Add chicken stock and toss over high heat to reduce liquid and cook the chicken. Thicken if needed.
5. Serve in an oval shallow casserole.
6. Garnish with Nuoc Mam, peanuts, banana blossom quarters, bean sprouts, powdered chile powder, sliced green chiles, and coriander.
